summary: Frontend-only end-to-end testing of Patricia Avila Sanchez Next.js portfolio. All 7 routes load with proper SEO metadata. Navigation, filters, work detail pages, mobile menu, 404 page and the contact form workflow (Formspree mocked) all work correctly. One real bug found: the contact form has `noValidate` and no JS-side required-field validation, so it submits empty payloads.

ui_bugs.issue: The <form> element has `noValidate` attribute AND handleSubmit() does not perform any client-side validation. As a result, clicking the submit button on an empty form fires a real POST request to Formspree and (when the endpoint returns 200, e.g. our mock) shows the 'Mensaje enviado' success state even though name/email/message were empty. Required fields are only enforced visually via the asterisk and HTML `required` attribute, but `noValidate` disables HTML5 validation. Recommendation: remove `noValidate` from the <form> tag (line 72 of /app/frontend/components/ContactForm.jsx) OR add explicit guard in handleSubmit (e.g. `if (!form.checkValidity()) { form.reportValidity(); return; }`).

issues: Dev-mode React hydration warning logged on the email anchor (`hola@patriciaavila.com`). Likely caused by browser/extension link rewriting; not reproducible in production builds. Worth verifying once after `next build && next start`.

action_items: Fix ContactForm validation: remove `noValidate` from <form> in /app/frontend/components/ContactForm.jsx (line 72) or add `form.checkValidity()` guard at the top of handleSubmit so empty submissions are blocked client-side.
critical_code_review_comments: ContactForm.jsx submits to Formspree without ANY client-side validation despite using `required` on inputs (because `noValidate` disables HTML5). Bug.
ContactForm.jsx uses `<style jsx>` with global selectors which is fine, but :global(.contact-input:focus) only changes border color — no aria-invalid feedback for accessibility.
WorkCard.jsx wraps the entire card in a <Link> which then includes external link helpers — fine, but ensure no nested <a> tags inside Markdown content for trabajos/[slug] cause hydration errors.
Next.js dev-mode 'Failed to fetch RSC payload' console error appears on initial hard navigation — it is a benign dev-server fast-refresh artefact; will not appear in production static export.
All interactive elements correctly carry data-testid attributes; good testability.
SEO is implemented correctly per page (unique <title>, meta description, og:title, og:description).
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None. Content read from /app/frontend/content/trabajos/*.md (6 files, all render).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only Next.js 14 App Router site. URL https://f85e3225-812f-4d4b-989c-5bf1912f6356.preview.emergentagent.com. trailingSlash:true on every route. To test the contact form without spamming Formspree, use Playwright `page.route('**/formspree.io/**', ...)` with `route.fulfill(status=200, body='{"ok":true}')`. All 6 work slugs: silencio-redaccion, memoria-fundacion-cultural, entrevista-cientifica, voz-editorial-banca, reportaje-envejecer, newsletter-farmaceutica. Categories used: Reportaje (2), Periodismo de Marca (2), Entrevista (1), Corporativo (1).
rca_of_the_issue: ContactForm bug RCA: <form> tag declares `noValidate` which tells the browser to skip the HTML5 constraint validation pipeline. Combined with handleSubmit() that does NOT call form.checkValidity() before fetching, no field is checked client-side. Reproduction: visit /contacto/, click 'Enviar mensaje' without filling anything, and (with a 200 response from Formspree) the success state appears. Mitigation: remove `noValidate` (line 72 ContactForm.jsx) OR add `if (!e.currentTarget.checkValidity()) { e.currentTarget.reportValidity(); return; }` at the top of handleSubmit before setStatus('submitting').
